title: Sprint 2 midpoint on technical refinement, reconciliation blockage, and provider data.

metadata.focus: Follow-up on sprint 2 with refinement of the retry fix and external blockage in reconciliation.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Mid-sprint. I want to see if we're really solving the problem or just getting a better understanding of the pain.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: The fix for the timeout has grown in scope. Just with exponential backoff it's not enough; we need to add an idempotency key and explicit retry tracing or we'll keep having weird cases.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: That changes the work quite a bit, but it makes sense. If you retry without idempotency, you can reduce the timeout and still leave inconsistent effects.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Okay, so the fix doesn't simplify: it expands to backoff plus idempotency key and retry tracing.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: With the duplicate charges, we have a blockage. Finance isn't giving us stable access to the settlement export and without that, we can't reconcile the reported cases properly.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: Without reliable export, we're going to keep looking at each case manually. That blocks any sort of semi-automated solution.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Agreed, that remains as an explicit blockage on the duplicate front.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: The provider comparison already has initial data. The current one performs worse on Monday mornings, but we still don't have a complete picture and I need to factor in operational cost.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: So, we're still comparing, but without a conclusion yet.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: If the Monday data is confirmed, we might need to prepare a controlled test with an alternative provider on a small part of the traffic.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Let's not decide to move traffic yet, but let's keep that option as a possible evaluation if the analysis continues pointing in the same direction.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: I would note it down already. Not as an immediate rollout, but as a potential technical canary.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: In the meantime, support is still handling duplicates manually and we can't promise a closure date to those customers.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Closing midpoint: the timeout fix is expanding in scope, duplicates are blocked by finance, the comparison remains open, and we note the possible controlled test with an alternative provider.

expected_items.title: Add idempotency key and retry traceability to the fix of the payment processing endpoint.
expected_items.summary: Extend the technical fix to avoid inconsistent effects and better track each retry.

expected_items.title: Evaluate a controlled test with partial traffic on an alternative provider.
expected_items.summary: Possible technical canary if the comparison continues to show degradation of the current provider.

expected_followups.matched_history_title: Correct the retry strategy of the payment processing endpoint with exponential backoff.
Analyze incidents of duplicate charges and define a minimal reconciliation.
Prepare a formal comparison of the current payment provider against two alternatives.
Keep the current provider during sprint 2 while the formal comparison is conducted.
